methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $41M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
First Resonance is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $40.8M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 9 private and 1 public company in the robotics and manufacturing sector, with particular focus on 6 peers at Series B stage and adjacent positions. Key comparables include 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), and Collaborative Robotics ($500M).

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised ($41M) as a proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implies a valuation of $82M. Peer-median methodology across the 6-company robotics cohort yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects material heterogeneity in company maturity and market position within the robotics sector.

Synthesizing both approaches produces a triangulated range of $69M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $578M. The stage-progression method yields the lower bound, reflecting First Resonance's Series A status; the peer median anchors the upper half. Key uncertainties include unit economics trajectory, nearness to Series B inflection, and differentiation versus the broader peer set, which spans from sub-$500M to decacorn valuations. A valuation in the $150M–$400M range may better reflect Series A positioning while acknowledging sector peer expansion.
